_scenario.title: Standards contributor proposes a new assembly type
_scenario.note: A proposal record rendered as JSON for illustration. The code 'FLR' is ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY — it is not in any dictionary, is not being proposed by this file, and must not be used in data. Real proposals follow proposals/TEMPLATE-code-change.md and docs/proposal-workflow.md.

proposal.title: Carton flow lane assembly type

fields_first_hurdle.why_it_fails: To be argued by the proposer: why a flow lane is not a shelving-unit configuration (inclined rollers, lane dividers, entry/exit geometry as distinct-role members) rather than fields on an existing type. If it CAN be fields, the proposal fails here — by design (P5).
fields_first_hurdle.why_not_a_field: required — empty submissions are returned at triage

proposal.evidence_required: At least two product families that would use the new assembly type; the component-vs-assembly test (§4.9) applied.
proposal.precedent_check: REJECTIONS.md searched; CFS/PIP/TUB precedents reviewed for analogy.

lifecycle.if_accepted: RESERVED row citing the proposal; ACTIVE only at the next snapshot release; decision recorded in proposals/
lifecycle.if_rejected: Recorded in REJECTIONS.md as binding precedent
lifecycle.if_deferred: Parked with a named revival condition and NO dictionary row (live example: proposals/2026-07-03-phase1-demo-alias-deviations.md)

refusals.message: The Explorer cannot mint codes at runtime; no code exists until a recorded decision activates it at a snapshot.
Variation representable as configuration fields is refused as a code, regardless of who proposes it.

explanations: The gate is evidence, not access: an outside engineer changes the standard through the public record, without knowing anyone.
Rejections become binding precedent, so the same argument is never re-run.
